bird.name: Lappet-faced vulture
bird.summary: The lappet-faced vulture or Nubian vulture (Torgos tracheliotos) is an Old World vulture belonging to the bird order Accipitriformes, which…

relations.subject: Lappet-faced vulture
relations.evidence: This vulture prefers to live in dry savannah, thornbush, arid plains, deserts with scattered trees in wadis, open mountain slopes
relations.object_summary: 地形开阔且便于大翼展鸟类活动的山地区域。

relations.subject: Lappet-faced vulture
relations.evidence: The declines are almost entirely due to human activities, including disturbances from habitat destruction and cultivation, disturbances at the nesting site (to which the species is reportedly quite sensitive) and ingestion of pesticides, which are usually set out for jackals and other small mammalian carnivores
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Lappet-faced vulture
relations.evidence: The declines are almost entirely due to human activities, including disturbances from habitat destruction and cultivation, disturbances at the nesting site (to which the species is reportedly quite sensitive) and ingestion of pesticides, which are usually set out for jackals and other small mammalian carnivores
relations.object_summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

relations.subject: Lappet-faced vulture
relations.evidence: It is suspected that they died after eating the corpses of 3 elephants that were poisoned by poachers, possibly to avoid detection by the birds, which help rangers to track poaching activity by circling above where there are dead animals. On 3 April 2022 the Israel Nature and Parks Authority announced the first hatching of a lappet-faced vulture since 1994 {{Citation |title=The first lappet-faced vulture to hatch in Israel in years checks out its new home
relations.object_summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。
